stash.Stash.reason: The constructor's option surface grows with every milestone (ttl at M3, limits at M4, onPopFailure at M5, tombstoneTtl at M7); today it accepts only backend and fail-loud-rejects the rest. Graduate once the full SPEC.md 4 option set is enforced rather than refused.

stash.push.reason: The verb's shape is final (SPEC.md 4) and its per-push options are now fully enforced (ttl at M3, reads at M5), with the limits that bound it landed at M4. Tombstone writes on early destruction (M7) still extend what a push must record. Graduate with the replication primitives.

stash.apply.reason: Read budgets (M5) landed and now route a budgeted apply through the claim/commit path -- the budget machinery is new this release. Graduate once it has soaked alongside the audit hook (M6) that inspects claim state.

stash.pop.reason: Ships at M5 with the claim/commit lifecycle, onPopFailure, and crash recovery. The read-and-destroy verb's shape is final (SPEC.md 4), but its recovery and claim-sharing machinery is new this release. Graduate once it has soaked alongside the audit hook (M6) that inspects claim state.

stash.store.reason: Ships at M7. The replication insert with the SPEC.md 4.4 six-check order, in-stream digest+size verification, and silent-by-contract emission is new this release; its convergence guarantees (eventually-once, no-resurrection) rest on the tombstone surface that lands alongside it. Pre-1.0 experimental until the option/error surface freezes for 1.0.

stash.tombstones.reason: Ships at M7. The grave shape ({ id, destroyedAt, cause }) and the four early-destruction causes are new this release; verify()'s tombstone-awareness and any stats() grave count are deliberate follow-ups, so the reconciliation surface is not frozen. Pre-1.0 experimental.

stash.show.reason: Metadata-only contract is settled and lazy expiry (M3) now returns an expired entry as RefNotFound. Pre-1.0 the whole surface stays experimental until the option set and error surface are frozen for 1.0.

stash.list.reason: includeExpired now has observable semantics (M3): the default filters expired entries and the option reveals them until a read verb reaps them. Pre-1.0 the surface stays experimental until frozen for 1.0.

stash.reconcilable.reason: The resilient anti-entropy source read: it returns healthy entries plus the ref ids of corrupt sidecars so one damaged entry cannot stall replication. The { entries, corrupt } shape rests on the replication surface (store, tombstones) still soaking pre-1.0; graduate once that surface freezes for 1.0.

stash.has.reason: Ships at M6. Existence query with the settled expired-as-false / InvalidRef-on-hostile / IntegrityError-not-absorbed contract; pre-1.0 the whole surface stays experimental until frozen for 1.0.

stash.stats.reason: Ships at M6. Aggregate counts (entries/bytes/claimed) over a fast physical walk; the claimed field's exactness rests on the M5 claim machinery still soaking. Pre-1.0 experimental until the option/error surface freezes for 1.0.

stash.verify.reason: Ships at M6. The finding-kind set and Report shape are new this release; M7 replication will extend the walk to tombstones and decide whether repair writes a tombstone, so the report contract is not frozen. Pre-1.0 experimental.

stash.drop.reason: Deletion semantics are final for a single store; tombstone writes on early destruction (M7) extend what drop must record. Graduate with the replication primitives.

stash.clear.reason: Same tombstone extension as drop at M7; the count-actual-destructions contract under concurrent removal is pinned by test. Graduate with stash.drop.

stash.prune.reason: prune() destroys expired entries on demand and returns the real destruction count. Its shape is settled (SPEC.md 4 janitorial bucket), but the sweep failure channel it feeds is silent until the 'sweepError' event lands at M6. Graduate once the event surface exists.

stash.close.reason: close() stops the sweep timer and Symbol.asyncDispose aliases it (SPEC.md 7.1). The contract is final, but disposal composes with the claim/crash-recovery lifecycle that arrives at M5. Graduate once the full lifecycle it bounds is in place.

stash.backends.DiskBackend.reason: The M2 surface (five methods, containment, hostile-sidecar rejection) is hardened, but the backend contract it implements grows its claim machinery at M5, verify at M6, and tombstones at M7 -- each reshapes what this class must hold on disk. Graduate once the full SPEC.md 9 method set ships and survives its milestones' vectors.

stash.backends.MemoryBackend.reason: The backend contract (SPEC.md 9) is exercised by one implementation; the claim/tombstone/verify surface it must grow arrives at M5-M7, and the disk backend (M2) is the second implementation that proves the conformance suite is backend-neutral. Graduate once both backends pass the full contract.
